unit_and_denominator: confirmed conversion events per callable complete meiosis and per callable informative-marker opportunity; report CO-associated and NCO-associated separately
minimum_samples_or_taxa: pilot: all 13 D01 complete tetrads; claim-grade transfer within the assayed cross: at least 50 independent complete meioses and at least 20 confirmed events
callable_opportunity: for each meiosis, the union of intervals in which both parental haplotypes differ at an informative marker and all four products pass depth, mapping, copy-number, segregation, and phase filters
identifiability_boundary: complete four-product segregation identifies a non-Mendelian 3:1/1:3 allelic conversion pattern and donor/recipient allele; events shorter than marker spacing are interval-censored and unobservable events are not imputed as counts
primary_model_and_null_simulation: beta-binomial or Poisson-binomial rate with meiosis-specific callable opportunity; simulate Mendelian tetrads under empirical depth/error, parental marker spacing, rearrangements, and phase/calling error; event truth requires reciprocal tetrad consistency
polarization_error_control: WS/SW direction follows known Col/Ler parental alleles and all four products; ambiguous/multiallelic or receptor-Cvi states are excluded, and W/S labeling is audited after strand normalization
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: context-stratified parental marker opportunities and reciprocal SW/WS error controls; no demographic model is relevant to a direct tetrad event count
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: CO breakpoints inferred from reciprocal haplotype switches; clustered errors and marker linkage preserved in chromosome-block simulations; meiosis is the independent unit
multiple_testing: one preregistered primary total-rate test; Holm correction across CO/NCO and direction strata; exploratory loci use BH q<=0.05 plus BY sensitivity
output_contract: per_meiosis_callable.tsv;events.tsv with event_id, donor, recipient, 3:1/1:3 state, CO class, inner/outer bounds, quality and reasons;rate_summary.tsv with exact intervals

unit_and_denominator: base pairs, interval-censored between innermost converted and flanking nonconverted informative markers
minimum_samples_or_taxa: at least 20 confirmed events for any distributional fit; otherwise list event bounds and median bounds only
callable_opportunity: events with two flanking informative markers on both sides and no mask discontinuity; single-marker events retain bounds but do not enter point-length fitting
identifiability_boundary: tract endpoints are not identified between markers; report Turnbull/nonparametric interval-censored distribution, never midpoint lengths as observations
primary_model_and_null_simulation: interval-censored survival likelihood; simulate tracts drawn on empirical marker/callability maps and pass through the caller; compare recovered vs injected distribution
polarization_error_control: parental allele direction as in D_EVT; no tract receives WS/SW label without an informative mismatch inside it
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: marker-density/context stratification; de novo mutation and sequencing-error mixtures estimated from Mendelian 2:2 sites
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: stratify CO-associated vs NCO; preserve chromosome and meiosis clustering in bootstrap
multiple_testing: Holm for CO-vs-NCO distribution contrasts; no genome-window p-values from event-poor pilot
output_contract: tract_summary.tsv with inner_bp, outer_bp, censoring class, CO class, bootstrap interval, and insufficient_events flag

unit_and_denominator: odds/rate ratio of confirmed conversion opportunity near CO versus matched non-CO opportunity; descriptive distance-to-CO distribution
minimum_samples_or_taxa: at least 20 confirmed conversion events and 20 meioses for a fitted association; D01 below threshold is descriptive only
callable_opportunity: callable informative bases in preregistered distance bins around CO breakpoints and chromosome/marker-density matched control intervals
identifiability_boundary: tetrads identify CO association at marker resolution, not molecular DSB association or causation
primary_model_and_null_simulation: conditional logistic/Poisson model matched by meiosis, chromosome, marker density, GC, and callability; null rotates CO labels within callable chromosome arms and re-runs detection
polarization_error_control: irrelevant for CO location; WS/SW subtype only after direct parental polarization
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: context and marker density covariates; no demographic inference
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: CO breakpoint uncertainty integrated; chromosome-arm permutations preserve recombination landscape
multiple_testing: one primary near-CO contrast; Holm across distance bins if more than one is interpreted
output_contract: crossover_association.tsv with opportunities, events, effect, interval, permutation p, and resolution bounds

estimand: GC transmission distortion among informative converted mismatches
unit_and_denominator: Pr(transmitted/converted allele is S | ancestral/donor-recipient pair is WS or SW), or S-versus-W converted count conditional on heterozygous W/S mismatches
minimum_samples_or_taxa: at least 100 independent informative W/S converted mismatches across at least 50 meioses for a claim-grade distortion estimate; otherwise exact binomial interval and NOT_ESTIMABLE_POWER flag
callable_opportunity: only directly polarized W/S mismatches inside confirmed conversion events where all four products and both parents are callable; one event is the cluster unit
identifiability_boundary: directly identifies biased resolution in this cross when thresholds pass; it does not identify a vertebrate-wide rate or explain historical GC content
primary_model_and_null_simulation: cluster-weighted binomial/mixed logistic model with one contribution per event sensitivity; null is 0.5 after conditioning on reciprocal detectability, simulated through context/depth/allele-balance error
polarization_error_control: known parental haplotypes and full tetrad establish direction; strand-complement normalization and reciprocal WS/SW spike-ins bound asymmetric errors
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: condition on 3-mer/5-mer, parental direction, allele balance, marker density, and callable S/W opportunities; demography is inapplicable
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: event/meiosis random effects; CO association is a covariate, not a substitute for direct bias
multiple_testing: one primary S-over-W test; familywise Holm for CO/NCO, parental-direction, and context sensitivities
output_contract: gc_transmission.tsv with informative event count, S and W counts, estimate, exact/cluster bootstrap interval, threshold status, and no_cross_species_extrapolation flag

estimand: polarized or polarization-aware WS-versus-SW derived allele-frequency spectra
unit_and_denominator: counts and normalized density per derived-count bin divided by context-specific callable monomorphic plus polymorphic opportunities
minimum_samples_or_taxa: at least 50 unrelated diploid individuals from one same-population stratum after QC; preregistered P01 YRI stratum targeted; replicate populations are separate strata
callable_opportunity: autosomal copy-two neutral sites callable in at least 95% of samples with ancestral-state posterior and matched 3-mer/5-mer opportunity; one SNP contributes once with genotype uncertainty
identifiability_boundary: detects a frequency-spectrum asymmetry compatible with gBGC after controls; does not identify parent-of-origin, conversion events, tract length, or transmission rate
primary_model_and_null_simulation: joint composite likelihood for WW/SS, WS, SW, and GC-conservative spectra under fitted demography; null sets B=0 while retaining mutation matrix, linked blocks, missingness, and polarization error; posterior predictive coalescent simulations
polarization_error_control: use two exact-version outgroups plus ancestral-state posterior; fit mispolarization epsilon by context and divergence; repeat folded analysis and exclude outgroup-discordant sites; never force ambiguous states
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: fit context-dependent mutation matrix from rare variants/de novo external sensitivity and jointly infer a flexible demographic history from GC-conservative sites; mask coding/conserved/selected sequence in primary; model ancestry/kinship and do not pool structured populations
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: block composite likelihood in recombination-map bins; LD-aware block bootstrap; stratify by recombination quintile using frozen sex-averaged map, with physical distance only as labeled proxy
multiple_testing: one primary WS-vs-SW spectrum contrast per population; Holm across preregistered populations; BH q<=0.05 for exploratory bins/annotations with block-level BY sensitivity
output_contract: population_sfs.tsv with raw/normalized bins and polarization posterior;population_qc.tsv;posterior_predictive.tsv; explicit NOT_RUN_DESIGN_ONLY in current synthesis

unit_and_denominator: B=4*N_e*b under the declared diploid diffusion convention, with posterior/likelihood interval; b and N_e are not separately identified from the SFS without external assumptions
minimum_samples_or_taxa: same n>=50 same-population design; require at least 1,000 callable polymorphic WS and 1,000 SW sites after all masks for a fitted B
callable_opportunity: context- and recombination-stratified WS/SW polymorphic and invariant opportunities passing P_SFS
identifiability_boundary: B is conditional on mutation, demography, linkage, structure, selection mask, and polarization model; it is not a direct tract rate, event count, or universal species property
primary_model_and_null_simulation: joint diffusion/composite-likelihood fit of demography, mutation nuisance, epsilon polarization, and B; null B=0; simulate genomes under fitted demography/recombination/mutation with and without B and pass through missingness/polarization pipeline
polarization_error_control: same mixture/folded/outgroup-discordance controls as P_SFS; epsilon uncertainty propagated rather than corrected once
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: GC-conservative spectra identify demography; 3-mer/5-mer mutation rates and rare-variant sensitivity identify mutation asymmetry; linked/background selection masks and alternative demographic families bound model dependence
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: empirical or pedigree recombination map frozen by digest; fit recombination strata; block jackknife at >=5-Mb or map-defined independent blocks; compare physical-distance proxy only as sensitivity
multiple_testing: likelihood-ratio calibration by parametric bootstrap; one primary B; Holm across populations/recombination interactions; report unadjusted intervals plus adjusted decision
output_contract: population_B.tsv with convention, nuisance parameters, interval, model family, posterior predictive checks, sensitivity envelope, and direct_rate=false

unit_and_denominator: N_WS/N_WS_opportunity versus N_SW/N_SW_opportunity and log rate ratio per branch/partition
minimum_samples_or_taxa: minimum 3 ingroup species plus 2 distinct outgroups; at least 10,000 callable neutral aligned bases and at least 20 WS plus 20 SW substitutions per interpreted branch/partition; otherwise descriptive counts only
callable_opportunity: reciprocal 1:1 single-copy orthologous aligned bases callable in all taxa needed for that branch; opportunity is ancestral W or S base in the same context and partition
identifiability_boundary: identifies long-term branch substitution asymmetry compatible with gBGC-like fixation bias; not direct meiotic events, current transmission distortion, or population B
primary_model_and_null_simulation: context-dependent nonstationary phylogenetic substitution model with branch parameters; null equal WS/SW fixation component after mutation opportunity; parametric simulations on fitted species/gene trees with empirical gaps, masks, GC, branch lengths, and annotation error
polarization_error_control: two-outgroup agreement or posterior ancestral-state integration; exclude discordant sites in primary; fit branch/context mispolarization; repeat transversion-only and high-posterior sensitivities
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: separate mutation opportunity by ancestral 3-mer/5-mer and fit GC-conservative rates; compare neutral, fourfold, coding, intronic, and intergenic partitions without treating coding as neutral; no present-day demographic parameter inferred
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: species-tree primary with multispecies-coalescent/local-gene-tree sensitivity, branch-length uncertainty, chromosome block jackknife, recombination proxies from linkage maps when available or syntenic GC/recombination covariates explicitly labeled proxies
multiple_testing: one primary neutral focal-branch contrast per H01/H02; Holm across two focal panels; branch/partition exploration BH q<=0.05 and BY sensitivity under dependence
output_contract: branch_substitution.tsv with opportunity/count/rate by context and partition;tree_sensitivity.tsv;polarization_qc.tsv;historical_only=true

unit_and_denominator: branch-specific fixation-bias/log-rate component or equilibrium GC* under the exact nonstationary model; never labeled population B without a population model
minimum_samples_or_taxa: H_SUB minima plus at least 100 total directionally informative substitutions on a branch for a fitted continuous bias parameter
callable_opportunity: same H_SUB callable opportunities, with branch and context assignment posterior
identifiability_boundary: model-identifiable historical bias only under the substitution/mutation/tree model; cannot be converted to events per meiosis, tract length, b, or 4N_e b
primary_model_and_null_simulation: fit mutation-selection-like nonstationary model where a gBGC-like coefficient changes WS/SW fixation; compare B-like coefficient zero by parametric bootstrap; simulate incomplete lineages and ILS
polarization_error_control: ancestral-state posterior integrated and epsilon sensitivity; require qualitative sign stability under both outgroups
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: context-specific mutation process, CpG-excluded primary and CpG-included sensitivity; neutral partition primary; coding selection kept separate
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: species-tree/gene-tree/topology and branch-length sensitivities; window/block bootstrap; recombination correlation is supporting heterogeneity only
multiple_testing: Holm across two primary focal branches; exploratory branches/partitions BH and BY sensitivity
output_contract: historical_bias.tsv with parameter name/convention, branch, partition, model, interval, null calibration, model-dependence warning, and not_direct_events=true

unit_and_denominator: cluster count/length and excess pair correlation relative to context/opportunity-matched null
minimum_samples_or_taxa: at least 50 branch-polarized WS substitutions and 20 Mb callable sequence for cluster inference; otherwise no clustering claim
callable_opportunity: high-posterior branch-assigned substitutions in continuous callable single-copy alignment segments
identifiability_boundary: clusters are candidate historical signatures; alignment error, multinucleotide mutation, hotspots, selection, and recurrent mutation can produce them, so clusters do not establish conversion tracts
primary_model_and_null_simulation: inhomogeneous Poisson/renewal null preserving context, chromosome, callable gaps, local mutation rate, and branch opportunity; parametric substitution simulations include multinucleotide events
polarization_error_control: retain only high-posterior branch assignments in primary and simulate inferred epsilon
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: context and local GC/mutation covariates; exclude coding/conserved primary
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: preserve chromosome/synteny blocks and fitted local rate; recombination proxy enrichment is secondary
multiple_testing: family of cluster scales corrected by maxT permutation; exploratory loci BH q<=0.05
output_contract: candidate_historical_clusters.tsv with null exceedance, alternative explanations, and candidate_only=true

unit_and_denominator: tract count and inner/outer length bounds per orthogroup/haplotype, conditional on resolved copy inventory
minimum_samples_or_taxa: at least 3 distinguishable paralog copies in 5 independent diploid individuals with the same copy-state class; claim of recurrence requires support in at least 3 independent individuals
callable_opportunity: copy-complete, uniquely flank-anchored paralog sequence with long-read/k-mer/read-depth support, copy-number state, gene tree, and no allelic collapse
identifiability_boundary: detects sequence homogenization more consistent with non-allelic conversion than the declared null; it is not allelic meiotic conversion, gBGC transmission, or a population B
primary_model_and_null_simulation: GENECONV-like maximal identical-fragment statistic on copy alignments; null permutes polymorphic-site labels conditional on copy tree, divergence, context, gaps, and tract-search space; coalescent/gene-tree simulations include recurrent mutation, unequal crossing-over, and assembly errors
polarization_error_control: copy donor/recipient direction is generally unidentifiable without a rooted copy tree and informative outgroup copy; unknown direction remains unknown; W/S bias is not inferred
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: context-dependent mutation and recurrent-mutation null; copy-age/gene-tree uncertainty; population demography not invoked unless multi-individual frequency is explicitly modeled
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: copy tree reconciled to species tree; physical linkage and tandem/interspersed class stratified; individual is the outer resampling unit and haplotypes remain nested
multiple_testing: one primary orthogroup family only if preregistered; maxT within orthogroup and Holm across orthogroups; exploratory tract scans BH plus permutation FDR

unit_and_denominator: observed within-species between-copy identity or gene-tree discordance minus copy-age/context-matched null
minimum_samples_or_taxa: at least 3 copies in 5 independent diploid individuals, with at least 10 independent orthogroups for a genome-wide comparison
callable_opportunity: resolved copy sequence and orthology/paralogy assignments; collapsed or copy-number-ambiguous haplotypes contribute missingness, not identity
identifiability_boundary: identifies homogenization among copies under a model; cannot distinguish gene conversion from selection or recurrent mutation without tract/gene-tree evidence and never establishes meiotic allelic gBGC
primary_model_and_null_simulation: phylogenetic mixed model plus copy-tree simulations without conversion; sensitivity adds conversion tracts, unequal crossover, recurrent mutation, and copy loss
polarization_error_control: root copy trees with outgroup copies when present; direction omitted when roots/copies are ambiguous
mutation_bias_and_demography_control: context-dependent mutation, gene constraint, expression/coding class, copy age, and GC matched; demographic claims forbidden
linkage_recombination_or_phylogeny_contr: copy/species-tree reconciliation and orthogroup random effects; tandem versus dispersed stratification; haplotypes nested within individual
multiple_testing: one genome-wide homogenization test; Holm for preregistered duplication classes; BH/BY for orthogroups
output_contract: paralog_homogenization.tsv with copy state, tree support, effect, null, alternatives, and non_allelic_only=true
